taxonomy.description: Business-correct meaning of entity state and accepted outcomes.
taxonomy.rule: Client must not invent or repair business semantics after the contract boundary.

taxonomy.description: Queues, process visibility, canonical rows, operational statuses and ordering.
taxonomy.rule: Client may paginate and render operational rows, but must not merge or normalize them as primary truth.

taxonomy.description: Money totals, balances, debt/overpayment split, overdue and critical amounts.
taxonomy.rule: Money facts and their semantics belong to typed backend contracts, never to selectors or screens.

taxonomy.description: Grouped report rows, export/PDF source rows, summary buckets and aggregates.
taxonomy.rule: If a screen or PDF consumes grouped or aggregated facts, backend owns that dataset.

taxonomy.description: Durable pending/inflight/retry/terminal lifecycle for offline-capable work.
taxonomy.rule: Queue/worker state may live locally, but never as ad hoc UI-owned orchestration.

taxonomy.description: Pure UI control state that does not alter business meaning.
taxonomy.rule: UI-only stores may own orchestration, but not truth-bearing rows, totals or statuses.

taxonomy.description: Formatting or color/label mapping that does not mutate the underlying truth.
taxonomy.rule: Presentation shaping is allowed only when it does not recalculate or reinterpret business facts.

mandatoryBackendOwned: finance totals, debt, overpayment, overdue and critical amounts
supplier finance aggregates and period/object-scoped finance facts
report summary rows and grouped facts
PDF/export source rows and canonical render datasets
pending proposal queue truth and deterministic ordering
clientOwnedAllowed: active tab and finance page mode
local presentation sort/filter that does not recalculate truth
file open/progress UI around PDF generation
notes: Director read/report/export contours are backend-primary now across finance, reports, PDF sources, and pending proposals.
No director residual client-truth families remain in this ownership map.

clientOwnedAllowed: temporary form values before durable sync
AI helper UI state and preview orchestration
notes: Foreman is primarily a write/runtime contour: durable local workflow state is allowed, but business submit truth belongs to RPC results and runtime classification.

mandatoryBackendOwned: inbox rows and next-page window data
summary counts/totals used by the business UI
clientOwnedAllowed: local UI filters and pure presentation sort mode

residualClientTruth.contour: Buyer summary buckets legacy client stitch fallback
residualClientTruth.currentClientOwnership: Buyer summary buckets still keep a legacy_client_stitch compatibility branch in the fetch layer.
residualClientTruth.whyRisky: Summary/report truth should not be restitched on device after the backend-first bucket scope already exists and is green.
residualClientTruth.requiredBackendContract: buyer_summary_buckets_scope_v1 hard cut with legacy stitch removal

notes: Buyer read-side contours are backend-primary; remaining risk sits in compatibility fallbacks inside fetchers.

residualClientTruth.currentClientOwnership: Payment PDF source branches still allow legacy_fallback alongside rpc_v1.
residualClientTruth.whyRisky: PDF/export truth should follow the same backend-owned source discipline as the interactive list contours.
residualClientTruth.requiredBackendContract: payment PDF source contract with rpc-only data branch

notes: Accountant is backend-first for lists and totals; residual debt is now limited to payment PDF fallback paths.

backendOwnedNow.owner: platform runtime + durable receive draft store

mandatoryBackendOwned: stock rows truth and canonical ordering
business-visible summary counters and PDF source rows

residualClientTruth.contour: Warehouse PDF source legacy fallback branches
residualClientTruth.currentClientOwnership: Warehouse PDF services still keep rpc_v1 | legacy_fallback source branches for report payloads.
residualClientTruth.whyRisky: Export/report source rows should converge on the same backend-owned scope discipline as queue and stock read models.
residualClientTruth.requiredBackendContract: warehouse PDF source family rpc-only cut

notes: Warehouse primary read contours are backend-owned and window-aware.
Residual work is about burning down legacy view/client-shaping compatibility branches, not reworking pagination or UI modernization.

residualClientTruth.contour: Contractor works bundle legacy enrich fallback
residualClientTruth.currentClientOwnership: Works bundle load service still carries legacy_client_enrich beside rpc_scope_v1.
residualClientTruth.whyRisky: Operational works truth is backend-owned primary already; legacy relational enrich remains a compatibility branch that keeps client composition alive.
residualClientTruth.requiredBackendContract: contractor_works_bundle_scope_v1 hard cut without legacy enrich

notes: Contractor reliability is runtime-owned now; residual risk remains in the legacy works-bundle enrich branch.

offlineSemantics.rule: Offline sync vocabulary and retry triggers are platform-owned runtime truth, not UI-owned logic.

guards.rule: Guard decisions and skip reasons are shared platform behavior; screens consume them but do not redefine them.

paginationWindowing.rule: Backend owns page/window truth and meta; client owns loadMore wiring, appendUnique discipline and render lifecycle only.

ownershipMarkers: ownership deltas captured in observability summaries

observability.rule: Ownership changes must stay machine-readable through observability artifacts; telemetry is platform discipline, not ad hoc screen logging.

forbidden: source meta duplicated in UI stores

durableWorkflowStoreAudits.purpose: durable local draft + sync/runtime telemetry, not UI orchestration
durable progress draft + retry/runtime state, not UI store
durable receive queue/runtime state, not UI store

stores.rule: Zustand UI stores are allowed only for orchestration. Durable workflow stores are runtime-owned and must not be treated as UI stores.

allowed: combining UI params with scope params
hooksControllers.rule: Hooks/controllers orchestrate; they do not become business compute nodes.

forbidden: new client compute layer for business truth
repositoriesServices.rule: Service/repository boundaries may adapt contracts narrowly, but cannot become new ownership islands for truth recompute.

forbidden: half-ready payloads that expect client recompute
backendContracts.rule: Critical read contracts must be versioned and sufficient for their screen or export contour.

pdfExportFlows.rule: PDF/export truth belongs to backend source families; client only orchestrates the render/open experience.

residualClientTruthRegistry.contour: Buyer summary buckets legacy client stitch fallback
residualClientTruthRegistry.currentClientOwnership: Buyer summary buckets still keep a legacy_client_stitch compatibility branch in the fetch layer.
residualClientTruthRegistry.whyRisky: Summary/report truth should not be restitched on device after the backend-first bucket scope already exists and is green.
residualClientTruthRegistry.requiredBackendContract: buyer_summary_buckets_scope_v1 hard cut with legacy stitch removal

residualClientTruthRegistry.currentClientOwnership: Payment PDF source branches still allow legacy_fallback alongside rpc_v1.
residualClientTruthRegistry.whyRisky: PDF/export truth should follow the same backend-owned source discipline as the interactive list contours.
residualClientTruthRegistry.requiredBackendContract: payment PDF source contract with rpc-only data branch

residualClientTruthRegistry.contour: Warehouse PDF source legacy fallback branches
residualClientTruthRegistry.currentClientOwnership: Warehouse PDF services still keep rpc_v1 | legacy_fallback source branches for report payloads.
residualClientTruthRegistry.whyRisky: Export/report source rows should converge on the same backend-owned scope discipline as queue and stock read models.
residualClientTruthRegistry.requiredBackendContract: warehouse PDF source family rpc-only cut

residualClientTruthRegistry.contour: Contractor works bundle legacy enrich fallback
residualClientTruthRegistry.currentClientOwnership: Works bundle load service still carries legacy_client_enrich beside rpc_scope_v1.
residualClientTruthRegistry.whyRisky: Operational works truth is backend-owned primary already; legacy relational enrich remains a compatibility branch that keeps client composition alive.
residualClientTruthRegistry.requiredBackendContract: contractor_works_bundle_scope_v1 hard cut without legacy enrich

priorityRoadmap.title: Buyer summary buckets fallback burn-down
priorityRoadmap.reason: Buyer summary buckets are already backend-primary; the remaining work is removing the legacy client stitch branch.

priorityRoadmap.title: Contractor works bundle fallback burn-down
priorityRoadmap.reason: Contractor runtime is reliable, but the legacy works enrich branch still keeps client composition semantics alive.

priorityRoadmap.title: PDF source family convergence
priorityRoadmap.reason: PDF/report fallback branches are narrower than live read contours, but should still converge on rpc-only source families.

intakeQuestions: Is this business truth, operational truth, money truth, report truth, queue/runtime truth, or only UI orchestration?
If it is truth-bearing, why is it not already on a backend-owned versioned contract?
Is there a typed boundary and explicit primary owner?
Does the change introduce new client-side aggregate, grouping, normalize, merge or reconcile logic?
If fallback exists, is it telemetry-visible and non-primary?
Is parity/runtime proof part of the batch?
Does any UI store start holding truth-bearing rows, totals or statuses?
forbiddenNewClientComputes: client-side report aggregate as primary truth
client-side operational merge as primary truth
client-side queue/retry truth in UI hooks or stores
enforcementRules.developmentRule: New backend-first batches must reference this ownership map and declare whether they burn down an existing residual or introduce only UI orchestration.
